item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Non-Euclidian just means "Geomotry that isn't on a perfectly flat plane", AKA anything on Earth (A sphere)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of sabotage with mnestics revealing a previously unknown anomaly is really cool. However, the article implies that the Site-112 staff have been covertly replaced with their parallel universe incarnations without the Foundation noticing, which is… well, a bit overdone, as an idea. (There are identical, burnt corpses of Site members, one desperately tries and fails to leave a message, and his parallel copy "doesn't remember" the results of taking a drug that forces you to remember.)

Once you notice that, you start to look for other flaws, and you find them. For example, when Dr. Iqbal spoke into a camera with no audio, why didn't the Foundation get a lip reader (or even a lip-reading AIAC?) to try and identify the spoken words?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So if I'm understanding this correctly, the massive dosage of mnestic caused everyone in site-112 to perceive a hidden dimension that was previously blocked out from their consciousnesses?

As for the rest of it, I thought the interrogation dialogue was really strong, but it feels like you need something to flesh out the actual impact of the events of the story.

item.title: Non-Euclidean Geometry
item.content:encoded:
I know I'm fighting a losing battle, and I won't vote based on this on this, but as someone who's taught Non-Euclidean Geometry, you are misusing it. There is nothing anomalous about Non-Euclidean Geometry. Nearly every object is Non-Euclidean in our universe. A three-dimensional universe should have 8 models of geometry, one Euclidean, and some mixed.

Look at the picture of Earth here
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-Euclidean_geometry#Elliptic_geometry

As from the actual scip, I'm unclear on what happened. Did everyone happen to have a drug trip while a parallel universe was hiding in site-112? 3937-A was a strong character, but nothing happens to him. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I-
what.

Why is the log suddenly dramatic and colloquial?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't decide if the use of a deep fried meme as a cognitohazard is stupid or wonderful. Actually, no—it's both.

Overall it's a fun, weird little incident with some very nice touches throughout. "MULTIVERSAL PANOPTICON OBJECT PRESERVATION SOCIETY" was probably my favorite bit. +1

item.title: What Happened to Site-112?
item.content:encoded:
My last-minute addition to JamCon's first day, a murder mystery wherein a whole Site is the victim and the inspector. I may try to return to this one day and actually give it the word-count it deserves.

Image is from here.

Thanks to Doctor Cimmerian and Waterfire for giving me some (extremely!) last-minute advice.

UPDATE: The incredible SunnyClockwork has created an illustration for this article, and it's stunning! Thank you so much!
